name: Trevor Lawrence
headline: Volume QB1 floor, but efficiency still lags
body:
Lawrence delivered his best fantasy season in 2025, ranking QB6 with 338.2 points across a full 17 games, a huge bounce from the injury-shortened 10-game, 145.2-point 2024. The counting stats carried it: 4,007 passing yards, 29 touchdowns against 12 picks, plus a career-best rushing line of 359 yards and nine scores on 82 carries that adds a sturdy floor. That ground production is the engine here, because the passing efficiency remains middling, a 60.9 percent completion rate, a negative 1.9 CPOE, and just a 0.089 EPA per play.

The peripherals temper the optimism: a 14.4 percent bad-throw rate, 78 poor throws, and 41 sacks taken behind shaky protection at a 21.8 percent pressure rate. He is more a product of volume and rushing equity than elite accuracy, but nine rushing scores and 560 attempts give him a dependable ceiling. Draft him as a low-end QB1 with upside if Jacksonville upgrades the line; a solid floor in superflex.
